dictationTips: What is the primary diagnosis causing mobility limitation?
How far can the patient walk? (e.g. less than 50m, 100m, cannot walk 400m)
Can they stand on a moving bus or train?
Can they negotiate the steps into/out of buses or trains? Can they manage a large flight of stairs?
Can they cross streets and kerbs safely?
What treatment have they had and what is the prognosis?
Is this permanent, or expected to resolve — and if so, within 12 months or longer?

mobilityFunctionalImpact.label: Functional Impact
mobilityFunctionalImpact.llmInstruction: Describe practical mobility restrictions and transport barriers. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

hasPhysicalDisabilities.label: Has Physical Disabilities
hasPhysicalDisabilities.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if physical disabilities affecting mobility are described. Default 'yes' since this form is about mobility.

physicalDisabilitiesDetails.label: Physical Disabilities Details
physicalDisabilitiesDetails.llmInstruction: Describe physical disabilities and how they affect mobility and ability to use public transport. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

q4Walking400m.label: Walking 400 metres
q4Walking400m.llmInstruction: Rate the patient's difficulty walking 400 metres: 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Infer from stated walking distance (e.g. cannot walk >100m → 4 or 5). Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q4StandingTransport.label: Standing on public transport
q4StandingTransport.llmInstruction: Rate difficulty standing on a moving bus or train: 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Lower limb OA and balance disorders typically impair standing on moving transport. Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q4SittingTransport.label: Sitting in public transport
q4SittingTransport.llmInstruction: Rate difficulty sitting in public transport: 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Most lower-limb or mobility conditions do not impair sitting (typically 1 or 2 unless severe hip/back pathology). Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q4CrossingStreets.label: Crossing streets and negotiating kerbs
q4CrossingStreets.llmInstruction: Rate difficulty crossing streets and stepping off kerbs: 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Relevant for lower limb weakness, balance issues, or visual impairment. Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q4NegotiatingSteps.label: Negotiating steps in/out of transport
q4NegotiatingSteps.llmInstruction: Rate difficulty negotiating the 1–2 steps into/out of buses or trains: 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Knee and hip pathology commonly affects this. Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q4FlightOfSteps.label: Negotiating a large flight of steps
q4FlightOfSteps.llmInstruction: Rate difficulty climbing a full flight of stairs (e.g. station overpass): 1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do. Most significant mobility impairments make this at least Serious (4) or Cannot do (5). Only set if patient has physical disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

hasPsychiatricDisabilities.label: Has Psychiatric/Intellectual Disabilities
hasPsychiatricDisabilities.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if psychiatric or intellectual disabilities are described. Default 'no'.

psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails.label: Psychiatric/Intellectual Details
psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails.llmInstruction: Describe psychiatric or intellectual disabilities and how they affect ability to use public transport. Keep to 2-3 short sentences — the text area is small.

q6PersonalSurvival.label: Personal survival skills
q6PersonalSurvival.llmInstruction: Rate personal survival skills (e.g. not a danger to self): 1=Full ability, 2=Slightly limited, 3=Moderately limited, 4=Severely limited, 5=No ability. Only set if patient has psychiatric or intellectual disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q6SocialSkills.label: Social skills
q6SocialSkills.llmInstruction: Rate social skills ability (e.g. ability to relate to bus drivers or public): 1=Full ability to 5=No ability. Only set if patient has psychiatric or intellectual disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q6EducationalSkills.label: Educational skills
q6EducationalSkills.llmInstruction: Rate educational skills ability (e.g. ability to handle money or buy tickets): 1=Full ability to 5=No ability. Only set if patient has psychiatric or intellectual disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

q6RecognitionSkills.label: Recognition skills
q6RecognitionSkills.llmInstruction: Rate recognition skills ability (e.g. ability to recognise landmarks or areas): 1=Full ability to 5=No ability. Only set if patient has psychiatric or intellectual disabilities. Leave blank if not enough information.

hasAnyDisabilities.label: Has Any Disabilities
hasAnyDisabilities.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if the patient has any physical, psychiatric, or intellectual disabilities. Almost always 'yes' for MA002.

hasOtherTransportInfo.label: Has Other Transport Info
hasOtherTransportInfo.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' if there is additional transport limitation information beyond physical and psychiatric. Default 'no'.

mobilityPermanentOrTemporary.label: Permanent or Temporary
mobilityPermanentOrTemporary.llmInstruction: Is the mobility limitation permanent or temporary?

mobilityDuration.label: Duration
mobilityDuration.llmInstruction: If temporary, will the mobility limitation last less than 12 months or 12 months or longer?

mobilityFoiRelease.label: Sensitive Information
mobilityFoiRelease.llmInstruction: Set 'yes' ONLY if specific information in this report could be prejudicial to the patient's mental or physical health if released to them directly. For routine conditions (e.g. osteoarthritis, mobility impairment), always 'no'.

systemPromptAdditions:
For MA002 emphasize mobility limitations, transport barriers, persistence of impairment, and current treatment response.

IMPORTANT: Each text area field maps to a small box on the PDF. Keep all text responses to 2-4 SHORT sentences maximum. Be concise and clinical.

PHYSICAL DISABILITIES (Q3–Q4):
- Extract physical disability description into 'physicalDisabilitiesDetails' (Q3 on PDF — describes the condition).
- Rate each Q4 activity field using the 1–5 scale (1=No difficulty, 2=Minor, 3=Moderate, 4=Serious, 5=Cannot do). Only set these if physical disabilities are present:
  - 'q4Walking400m' (Q41): infer from stated walking distance — cannot walk 100m → 4 or 5; walking impaired but can manage 400m → 2 or 3
  - 'q4StandingTransport' (Q42): lower limb OA and balance disorders typically make standing on moving transport Serious (4) or worse
  - 'q4SittingTransport' (Q43): most lower-limb conditions do NOT impair sitting — default to 1 or 2 unless hip/back pathology explicitly limits sitting
  - 'q4CrossingStreets' (Q44): relevant for lower-limb weakness, deformity, or visual impairment
  - 'q4NegotiatingSteps' (Q45): knee and hip pathology commonly makes negotiating bus/train steps Serious (4) or Cannot do (5)
  - 'q4FlightOfSteps' (Q46): significant lower-limb impairment almost always makes a full flight of stairs Serious (4) or Cannot do (5)
  - Leave any Q4 field blank if the narrative does not provide enough information to rate it confidently.

PSYCHIATRIC/INTELLECTUAL DISABILITIES (Q5–Q6):
- If mentioned, set 'hasPsychiatricDisabilities' to 'yes' and extract details into 'psychiatricDisabilitiesDetails'.
- Rate each Q6 skills field using 1–5 (1=Full ability, 2=Slightly limited, 3=Moderately limited, 4=Severely limited, 5=No ability). Only set if psychiatric disabilities are present:
  - 'q6PersonalSurvival' (Q61): personal survival skills — not a danger to self
  - 'q6SocialSkills' (Q62): social skills — ability to relate to drivers or public
  - 'q6EducationalSkills' (Q63): educational skills — handling money, buying tickets
  - 'q6RecognitionSkills' (Q64): recognition skills — recognising landmarks or areas
  - Leave any Q6 field blank if not enough information.

OTHER TRANSPORT INFO (Q7):
- Set 'hasOtherTransportInfo' to 'yes' and populate 'mobilityFunctionalImpact' if there is additional transport-specific information beyond what is in Q3/Q5.

RELEASE OF INFORMATION (Q10):
- Set 'mobilityFoiRelease' to 'no' for all routine conditions (osteoarthritis, fractures, neurological conditions, etc.).
- Only set to 'yes' if the clinical notes explicitly indicate information in this report could harm the patient's mental or physical health if disclosed directly to them. This is very rare.

dictationGuide.title: Condition & Prognosis
dictationGuide.description: Indicate the nature and duration of the mobility limitation.

dictationGuide.title: Clinical Narrative
dictationGuide.description: Describe the patient's mobility condition, how it affects their ability to use public transport, and current treatment.

questions.label: Mobility & Transport Impact
questions.placeholder: e.g. Severe bilateral knee OA with valgus deformity. Cannot walk >100m without rest — cannot walk 400m. Unable to stand on moving transport. Cannot negotiate bus steps without gripping rail — serious difficulty. Sitting is comfortable. Awaiting TKR. Temporary — expected resolution >12 months.
